compiled_state.title: [Review Workflow]: Orchestrator skips tier classifier and hardcodes light tier -> review recorded without classifier telemetry
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

Per REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and CLAUDE.md, the review tier must be determined by dispatching the classifier agent before dispatching any reviewer. The classifier output (`selected_tier`) is what determines whether to use light (haiku), standard (sonnet), or deep (3×sonnet + opus) review. The orchestrator must never hardcode a tier.

#### Actual Behavior

When a staged diff's hash changed due to adding a 1-line config entry (`.claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-tickets-boundary-allowlist.conf`), the orchestrator reasoned: "this is a trivial 1-line config change, it should be light tier" and dispatched `dso:code-reviewer-light` directly without running the classifier.

The resulting `reviewer-findings.json` lacked a `selected_tier` field, triggering the record-review.sh warning: "selected_tier not found in reviewer-findings.json or classifier-telemetry.jsonl — cannot verify tier; allowing review (fail-open)."

#### Rationalization That Was Applied

The orchestrator observed that the allowlist change was a single-line addition to a config file with no logic. It concluded the tier was "obviously" light and skipped the classifier as an optimization. This is exactly the rationalization the classifier gate is designed to prevent — the classifier exists to make tier decisions impartially, not to be skipped when the orchestrator thinks the answer is obvious.

#### Contract Reference

CLAUDE.md "Never Do These" rule 8: "Never manually call `record-review.sh`" — the review gate requires the classifier-selected code-reviewer sub-agent(s) to write the findings. Rule 11: "Never override reviewer severity." The tier selection discipline is part of the same integrity boundary.

The review workflow (REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 1) requires classifier dispatch before reviewer dispatch, regardless of perceived diff complexity.

comments.body: Fixed: Added 'No diff is exempt from Step 3' warning to REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 3, explicitly calling out 1-line config changes and 'obviously trivial' diffs as the rationalization failure mode being prevented. Also references the incident (e288-04f0) so the rule is traceable. The classifier shell command is now described as a mandatory gate, not an optimization.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fixed: added No-diff-is-exempt warning to REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 3 for the classifier gate.
comments.author: Test
